chunk_summary: Stunting, a marker of chronic undernutrition, is defined by height-for-age below -2 SD from WHO standards. India faces a high burden, with NFHS-5 reporting significant prevalence among children under 5 years and CNNS adding biomarker insights.
learning_objectives: Understand the epidemiologic definition of stunting and its classification criteria.
Analyze the national burden of stunting and chronic undernutrition in India using NFHS-5 and CNNS data.
Identify determinants of growth faltering and differentiate stunting from other phenotypes like wasting or edema.
key_facts: Stunting is defined as height-for-age below -2 SD from WHO child growth standards; severe stunting is < -3 SD.
NFHS-5 (2019–21) reports high prevalence of stunting, wasting, and anemia among children under 5 years in India.
CNNS (2016–18) provides biomarker data on micronutrient deficits and inflammation pathways.
Correct measurement techniques include recumbent length for children under 2 years and standing height thereafter.
For children older than 5 years, height-for-age and BMI-for-age should be interpreted using WHO 5–19 year growth reference or IAP charts.
reference_quote: NFHS-5 (2019–21) reports a high national burden among children under 5 years, with stunting and wasting remaining common and anemia extremely prevalent in 6–59 month children.
document_key_topics: National burden of stunting in India
